defs: The position or rank of a yeoman.
The collective body of yeomen, or freeholders.
quotes: The enfranchised yeomanry began to feel an instinct for dominion.

defs: A British volunteer cavalry force, growing out of a royal regiment of fox hunters raised by Yorkshire gentlemen in 1745 to fight the Pretender, Charles Edward; -- called also yeomanry cavalry. The members furnish their own horses, have fourteen days' annual camp training, and receive pay and allowance when on duty. In 1901 the name was altered to imperial yeomanry in recognition of the services of the force in the Boer war. See Army organization, above.
